text:
There is **no structured trace/receipt** tying a specific published status event to its cause. What exists:

- `src/distill.rs:20-37` `dlog()` — appends to **one shared file** `~/.tenex-edge/logs/distill.log`, one line per distill *attempt/response*, keyed by an 8-char session-id prefix (collision-prone across sessions). Lines like `"calling {provider}/{model}"`, `"distill response: {out:?}"`, `"falling back to nudge-to-keep current_title={current_title:?}"` (`distill.rs:379-412`).
- `src/runtime.rs:33-51` `slog()` — appends to a **per-session file** `~/.tenex-edge/logs/<session_id>.log`. Logs distill scheduling/completion only: `"[distill] due transcript_path=..."` (`runtime.rs:351`), `"[distill] spawning task ctx_len=... current_title=..."` (`runtime.rs:362`), `"[distill] task finished result=... error=..."` (`runtime.rs:297-299`), `"[distill] applied title=..."` (`runtime.rs:306`), `"[distill] ERROR: ..."` (`runtime.rs:318`).
- **Nothing logs the actual publish event itself on success.** `enqueue_status` (`runtime.rs:140-173`) and `spawn_status_heartbeat_publisher` (`status_publish.rs:76-118`) only call `tracing::error!` on *failure* (e.g. `status_publish.rs:109-113` `"status_publish: set_status failed — presence not refreshed this tick"`). A successful heartbeat re-publish, turn-end publish, or startup publish produces **zero log output**.
- No event correlates a published `kind:30315`'s `content` back to which of the 5 trigger paths (startup / per-engine heartbeat / turn-end / distill-applied / daemon heartbeat re-arm) produced it, nor to the transcript snippet that was fed to the LLM.
